---
name: Pull Request
about: Submit a code change for review
---

## What does this PR do?

<!-- One paragraph. What problem does this solve? What does it change? -->

## Type of Change

- [ ] `feat` — New feature
- [ ] `fix` — Bug fix
- [ ] `refactor` — Code restructuring (no behavior change)
- [ ] `docs` — Documentation only
- [ ] `chore` — Maintenance, dependencies, config
- [ ] `test` — Tests only
- [ ] `perf` — Performance improvement

## Related Issue

Closes #<!-- issue number -->

## Testing

<!-- What did you do to verify this works? -->

- [ ] Tested locally
- [ ] Added/updated unit tests
- [ ] Tested on mobile viewport
- [ ] Tested error states

## Screenshots (if UI change)

<!-- Before / After screenshots -->

## Self-Review Checklist

- [ ] My code follows the project's coding standards
- [ ] I have not hardcoded any values that should be environment variables
- [ ] All new API endpoints have input validation
- [ ] All new UI components handle loading, error, and empty states
- [ ] I have not introduced any `any` types in TypeScript without justification
- [ ] The commit message follows Conventional Commits format
- [ ] I have reviewed my own diff before submitting
